page: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by a custom 16-core Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyper-threading resource contention. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, alongside a bundled 945 GB local storage disk.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory subsystem capabilities, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top-tier category. Large-block memory bandwidth tests show read speeds of 312.21 GB/sec and write speeds of 125.75 GB/sec, both ranking in the strong top 25% tier. Computational performance is solid, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks scoring in the average range. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also demonstrate strong performance, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput maintain average tier results.

While the ARM-based architecture delivers high memory bandwidth and efficient multi-threaded execution, it exhibits limitations in specific workloads. For instance, large language model inference with a medium 7B model shows weak prompt processing speeds of 24.69 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average at 14.15 tokens/sec. Given the balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, high-speed local storage, and strong memory bandwidth, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, web serving, and software compilation tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high memory throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 16-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Featuring a dedicated core architecture with one thread per core, it delivers 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 945 GB local storage disk. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth. While single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks sit in the average tier, the instance excels in memory-intensive operations and multi-threaded decompression. However, it shows weaker performance in complex tasks like medium-model LLM prompt processing. This instance is highly suitable for database hosting, web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications requiring a balanced resource profile and high memory throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v6 is a 16-core ARM Neoverse-N2 virtual machine with 64 GB RAM, 945 GB local storage, and top-tier memory performance, making it highly suitable for database and general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v6 is a 16-core ARM Neoverse-N2 virtual machine featuring 64 GB RAM, 945 GB local storage, and high memory bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for balanced enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 945 GB local storage disk
Delivers top-tier memory performance and strong large-block read/write bandwidth
Features dedicated physical cores with one thread per core to prevent resource contention
Optimized for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing
